name: High Privilege User Performing Suspicious Actions
notes: This rule triggers when a user with high privileged role(e.g. Administrator) performs suspicious activities. This action can indicate a user changing the permissions to perform malicious actions or accessing unauthorized machines.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
when any of Username, Target Username (custom), User ID (custom) are contained in any of Users with High Privilege Role Names - AlphaNumeric
TD_list: APPLY High Privilege User Performing Suspicious Actions on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Virtualization Activities
AND when any of Username, Target Username (custom), User ID (custom) are contained in any of Users with High Privilege Role Names - AlphaNumeric
RED_list: Event name: High Privilege User Performing Suspicious Actions
Event description: User who achieved higher privilege performed suspicious activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
